title: Breadth-first search shortest path length in an unweighted directed graph

informal_statement.text:
Given an unweighted directed graph represented by an adjacency list of n vertices and a source vertex, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is Some(k) if the shortest path from source to v uses k edges, and None if v is unreachable.

Benchmark note (graph_bfs_shortest_path_006): BFS distance table (variant 6): emphasize self-loops and zero-length cycles at the source.
Authoring lens: Lens 6 checks minimality clauses remain meaningful when the graph has redundant edges.
preconditions: every neighbor index is in [0, n)
required_properties: if dist[v] == Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges
if dist[v] == Some(k), no directed path from source to v uses fewer than k edges
if dist[v] == None, there is no directed path from source to v
edge_cases: graph with a single vertex and no edges
